cot_prefix: Okay, I need to determine if the sentence "Petr Cech was safe at first." is plausible. Let me start by understanding who Petr Cech is. Petr Cech is a Czech former professional footballer. He is known for being a goalkeeper, most notably for playing for Chelsea FC in the English Premier League and winning multiple Premier League titles and a Champions League. He has also played for AC Milan, Arsenal, and was part of the Czech national team. He is considered one of the best goalkeepers of his generation. Now, the sentence in question is "Petr Cech was safe at first." This is a short sentence, so it could be a fragment of a longer narrative or explanation. The phrase "at first" suggests a comparison to a later time, implying that something might have happened to change his situation. Let me analyze the sentence for plausibility: 1. "Petr Cech" - This is a specific and well-known individual, so using his name is fine. 2. "was safe" - This phrase is a bit ambiguous without more context. "Safe" could mean: - Physically safe or unharmed - In a secure or protected position - In a safe or vault (though this is less likely for a person) - In a safe place or situation 3. "at first" - This implies that his state of being "safe" was only temporary, and something changed
